title: str2hax
requirements: A Wii on System Menu version 4.3 (any region)
An SD card, any size up to 32GB (for BootMii and Priiloader — str2hax itself doesn't need one)
A Wii Remote and sensor bar
An internet connection on the Wii
warnings: Every time you modify a Wii there is a !!small chance of an unrecoverable brick!!. Follow each step exactly.
!!Do NOT!! follow old video tutorials — they often recommend outdated software that can cause bricks.
Make a NAND backup when the guide tells you to. Don't skip it — it's your only recovery if something goes wrong.
_note: str2hax is an exploit that works through the Wii's EULA agreement page by changing your DNS settings. No SD card needed for the exploit itself — BootMii and Priiloader still need one later.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
This guide installs the **Homebrew Channel**, **BootMii**, and **Priiloader** on your Wii — the full set of modern homebrew tools. The exploit itself (**str2hax**) works through the Wii's EULA page, so you don't even need an SD card to get started. Once the Homebrew Channel is installed, Graphite helps you set up the SD card for everything else.

Graphite handles file downloads, SD card setup, and even saves your NAND backup to the Desktop. The only things you do on the Wii are button presses and navigating menus — I'll walk you through each one with a picture.

First, I'll check that your SD card is FAT32 (if you don't have one yet, get one — you need it for BootMii).

steps.title: Put the Priiloader files on your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads the **Priiloader Installer** and **Load Priiloader** and unpacks them into the right `apps` folders on your SD card. These are the tools you'll use later to install brick protection.

This is everything the guide would normally have you download by hand and drag over. Just press the button and wait.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Move the SD card to your Wii
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of your computer and slot it into the **SD card slot on the front of the Wii**. A USB adapter won't work — it must be the built-in slot.

Make sure the console is connected to the internet. Press **I did this** once the card is in.

steps.title: Change your Wii's DNS settings
steps.body:
str2hax works by redirecting the Wii's EULA page to a server that delivers the exploit. You need to change the DNS on your connection.

1. From the Wii Menu, go to **Wii Options** → **Wii Settings** → **Page 2** → **Internet** → **Connection Settings**.

2. Select your connection slot and choose **Change Settings**.

3. Set **Auto-Obtain DNS** to **No**, then click **Advanced Settings**.

4. Set the **Primary DNS** to `3.143.163.250`. Leave the Secondary DNS blank.

5. Click **Confirm**, then **Save**, then **OK** to run the connection test. If it passes, select **No** to skip the system update.

If the test fails with error `521xx`, double-check the DNS number.

steps.title: Trigger the exploit
steps.body:
Back in **Internet** settings, select **User Agreements** (or **Agreement/Contact**) and choose **Yes**.

Click **Next**. If the exploit server is working, you'll see the screen in the first picture — a custom EULA page. If you see the normal Nintendo EULA instead, your ISP blocks custom DNS and you'll need a different method.

!!Do NOT press I ACCEPT or I DO NOT ACCEPT!!. Wait 1–2 minutes. The exploit downloads automatically. If it works, the second screen (the scam warning) appears — that's the **HackMii Installer** loading.

If nothing happens after 2 minutes, or you see `Hanging.` or `ERROR!`, restart the Wii and try again.

steps.title: Install the Homebrew Channel
steps.body:
You're now in the **HackMii Installer**. The first screen is the scam warning — just a reminder not to pay for free software.

1. Wait 30 seconds for **Press 1 to continue** to appear, then press **1** on your Wii Remote.
2. You'll see the system test results. Select **Continue**.
3. Select **Install the Homebrew Channel**. It installs in a few seconds.
4. When the green tick appears, select **Continue**.

steps.title: Install BootMii
steps.body:
Still in the HackMii Installer, select **BootMii...**.

Select **Install BootMii as IOS**. Wait for the progress bars to complete. When the green tick appears, select **Continue**.

!!If you see the option to Install BootMii as boot2!! — this gives stronger brick protection. Install that too. (Most Wiis won't show this option; yours was made before 2009 if it does.)

Finally, select **Return to the main menu**, then **Exit**. The console boots into the **Homebrew Channel**.

steps.title: Launch BootMii
steps.body:
You're now at the **Homebrew Channel** — a grid of homebrew apps. Before installing Priiloader, make a NAND backup so you can recover from any future problems.

Press the **HOME** button on your Wii Remote, then select **Launch BootMii**.

(If the button doesn't appear, BootMii wasn't installed — re-run the exploit and install it again.)

You should see BootMii's main menu (the second picture). Use the **GameCube controller's D-pad** or the **console's POWER/RESET buttons** (POWER = move right, RESET = select).

steps.title: Make a NAND backup
steps.body:
This is !!your safety net!!. If anything ever goes wrong, this backup can bring the Wii back.

In BootMii, select the **Options** button (the gears icon). Then select the first icon on the left (NAND Backup).

BootMii dumps your NAND and shows the progress. "Bad Blocks" are normal — they come from the factory. When the dump finishes, BootMii verifies the backup. Ideally all blocks are green.

Press the **Back** button (arrow icon), then select either the **Wii Menu** or **Homebrew Channel** to exit BootMii.

Power off the Wii.

troubleshoot.label: The backup failed or has errors

steps.title: Save your NAND backup
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of the Wii and plug it back into this computer.

Press the button and Graphite grabs the backup the console just made (`nand.bin` and `keys.bin`), copies it into a folder on your **Desktop**, and clears the files off the SD card to free up space. If a backup folder already exists, it saves a new one alongside it.

This is optional; you can skip ahead with **Next**, but keeping the backup is strongly recommended.

steps.title: Install Priiloader
steps.body:
Priiloader is your brick protection — it loads before the System Menu, so even if the menu is corrupt, you can recover. Put the SD card back in the Wii and power it on.

1. Launch the **Homebrew Channel**.
2. Open **Priiloader Installer** from the list.
3. Press the **+ Button** on your Wii Remote (or **A** on a GameCube controller).
4. Wait for the install to complete, then press **A** to return to the Homebrew Channel.
5. Press **HOME**, then select **Exit to System Menu**. The console will load Priiloader's menu automatically.

steps.title: Configure Priiloader
steps.body:
Now enable the system menu hacks that keep your Wii safe:

1. In Priiloader, scroll down to **System Menu Hacks** and press **A**.
2. Enable the following:
   - **Block Disc Updates** (stops game discs from forcing updates)
   - **Block Online Updates** (stops Nintendo from force-updating you)
   - If you play in 480p: **480p graphics fix in the system menu**
   - If you're not on a CRT: **Remove Deflicker** (makes text sharper)
3. Scroll to **save settings** and press **A**.
4. Press **B** to go back, then select **System Menu**.

From now on, hold **RESET** while powering on to enter Priiloader. You're protected.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Your Wii has the **Homebrew Channel**, **BootMii**, and **Priiloader** installed. You're fully protected and ready to run homebrew apps.

Drop homebrew apps into the `apps` folder on your SD card and launch them from the Homebrew Channel. For USB loaders, cIOS, or restoring online services, check the guides at `wii.hacks.guide`.

To enter Priiloader in the future: hold **RESET** while powering on. To restore your NAND: launch BootMii from the Homebrew Channel or use the BootMii recovery guide.
